title: Vessel with improved stability and method of navigation
abstract: The invention relates to a vessel, consisting of a gondola (1), propelling torpedoes (2) with electric motors (3) and flaps (4) for steering, in which the propelling torpedoes (2) are connected to the gondola (1) by means of connecting arms (5) that comprise regulating means (6) for the distance between the gondola (1) and the propelling torpedoes (2), there being a control system to measure the waves and control the propelling torpedoes (2), the flaps (4) and the regulating means (6) of the connecting arms (5). The invention also refers to a method of navigation of an aero-submarine vessel in which, by acting on a control system on regulating means (6), the distance between the gondola (1) and the propelling torpedoes (2) being regulated up to a certain value at which the propelling torpedoes (2) are submerged and the gondola (1) is arranged above the waves.
claims:
Claims 1. Vessel, which includes - a self-buoyant gondola or gondola (1) configured to accommodate crew, passage, and merchandise, - propelling torpedoes (2) under the gondola, connected to said gondola (1), and aligned longitudinally with it, submersible under water, in which electric motors (3) are arranged, - depth control means for the propelling torpedoes (2), - azimuth thrusters (8) arranged in the gondola (1), - and main batteries connected to the electric motors (3) and azimuth thrusters (8), characterized by - the propelling torpedoes (2) are connected to the gondola (1) by means of connecting arms (5) that comprise regulating means (6) for the distance between the gondola (1) and the propelling torpedoes (2), - the vessel comprising a control system configured to measure the waves and control and operate the propelling torpedoes (2), the depth control means and the regulating means (6) for the distance between the gondola (1) and the propelling torpedoes (2). 2. Vessel according to claim 1, wherein the depth control means comprise a plurality of flaps (4) configured for tilting the propelling torpedoes (2) up and down. 3. Vessel, according to claim 2, comprising horizontal bars (7) connectors of the propelling torpedoes (2) in which the flaps (4) are arranged. 4. Vessel, according to claim 1, wherein the depth control means comprise tilting means (9) of the propelling torpedoes (2) with respect to the connecting arms (5). 5. Vessel, according to any of the preceding claims, in which the regulating means (6) for the distance between the gondola (1) and the propelling torpedoes (2) are selected from a rack-and-pinion system, friction rollers, telescopic connecting arms (5) system, articulated connecting bars (6) system, scissor-articulated connecting bars (6) system, or alternative systems of controlled displacement of a profile under load. 6. Vessel, according to any of the previous claims, in which the gondola (1) is made of composite. 7. Vessel, according to claim 6, wherein the gondola (1) comprises a sandwich structure that in turn comprises flax fibers, reinforcing carbon fibers and epoxy bio resins laminated in a vacuum and post-cured in an oven. 8. Vessel, according to any of claims 1 to 5, wherein the gondola (1) is made of light metal alloys. 9. Vessel, according to claim 8, in which the light metal alloys are aluminum or titanium and comprise a structure of transverse rings and longitudinal reinforcements. 10. Vessel, according to any of the preceding claims, comprising two propelling torpedoes (2) and an electric motor (3) arranged at each of the ends of the propelling torpedoes (2). 11. Vessel, according to any of claims 1 to 9, comprising four propulsion torpedoes (2), and an electric motor (3) arranged in each of the torpedoes (2). 12. Vessel, according to any of the previous claims, in which the batteries are lithium ion. 13. Vessel, according to any of the previous claims, which It includes battery charging means, selected from diesel or LNG generators, hydrogen fuel cells, solar panels, shore power chargers, and combination thereof. 14. Vessel, according to any of the previous claims, which includes auxiliary batteries. 15. Method of navigation of the vessel, the vessel being of the type that comprise a gondola (1) connected to propelling torpedoes (2) by connecting arms (5), which comprises the following stages: 

- at the beginning of navigation and maneuver in port, propulsion and navigation is carried out using azimuth thrusters (8) arranged in the gondola (1), keeping the propelling torpedoes (2) out of the water, up to a certain vessel speed, - tilting of the propelling torpedoes (2) to submerge them under water by means of depth control means actuated by a control system, up to a certain depth of the propelling torpedoes (3), - wave measurement by the control system, - action by the control system of regulating means (6) of the length of the connecting arms (5) up to a certain length in which the propelling torpedoes (2) are submerged and the gondola (1) is arranged above the waves, - navigation through the action of the propelling torpedoes (2) and the depth control means, - stopping the propelling torpedoes (2) and raising them above the water by activating the depth control means, - at the end of the navigation and maneuver in port, propulsion and navigation is carried out using the azimuth thrusters (8) arranged in the gondola (1) keeping propelling torpedoes (2) out of the water until the vessel stops.
